notes: Examine.com evidence-based summary; 38+ references, 22 clinical trials analyzed; covers adaptogenic stress reduction, fatigue, and cognitive function; last updated Dec 2025
2012 systematic review; 11 qualifying RCTs; finds some evidence for physical and mental fatigue benefit but notes methodological weaknesses across trials; by University of Alberta
2022 review (PMC9228580); covers Rhodiola rosea preparations for life-stress symptoms including anxiety, burnout, exhaustion; encouraging clinical evidence with good safety profile
2024 review of Rhodiola as adaptogen for exercise performance; covers 16+ human investigations; summarizes ergogenic potential with ~200mg acute dosing pre-exercise
Article is about Rosa damascena (Damask rose), not Rhodiola rosea — completely wrong plant
